labels: Mild to moderate left pleural effusion is decreased in size from the prior exam has as is adjacent left basal atelectasis. No evidence of pneumothorax.
report:
=== VISUAL_FINDINGS ===
Mild left basal atelectasis, marked thoracolumbar dextroscoliosis, clear right lung, tiny coils projecting over the medial right hemithorax.

=== CLINICAL_EVIDENCE ===
Retrieved Literature: 
- FINDINGS: Mild to moderate cardiomegaly is stable from the prior examination. There has been an interval decrease in adjacent left basal atelectasis. The right lung is clear. No evidence of pneumothorax. Marked thoracolumbar dextroscoliosis is unchanged.
- IMPRESSION: Mild to moderate left pleural effusion is decreased in size from the prior exam has as is adjacent left basal atelectasis. No evidence of pneumothorax.
- FINDINGS: Moderate cardiomegaly has been stable compared to exams dated back to. There has been an interval increase in opacification of the right lung base and a small right pleural effusion with adjacent atelectasis. Small left pleural effusion is persistent. There is no evidence of a pneumothorax. There is mild pulmonary vascular congestion and mild edema. The visualized osseous structures are unremarkable.
- IMPRESSION: Interval progression of opacification
